observations: The canonical three-step plan has zero network, credential, model, social, skill, or paid-call authority and stops at an exact artifact-version creator gate.
The append transaction validated event/catalog payloads, advanced aggregate versions, extended the per-workspace hash chain, and updated projections atomically in SQLite WAL mode.
Boot verified stored row/envelope hashes and rebuilt disposable projections before recovery; a tampered event row prevented the Harness from opening canonical state.
The exact compiled plan was embedded in run.planned and its contract, run/workspace/project scope, and self-excluding content hash were revalidated before recovery execution.
A repeated command with the same idempotency key returned the original run without appending events; changed command content under that key failed closed.

Projection deletion and event replay reproduced the same workspace, project, artifact, run, and step snapshot without deleting the retained checkpoint; duplicate artifact-version projection and cross-workspace run reads failed closed.
A deterministic step interrupted while running recovered on the second attempt after process restart and created exactly one brief artifact.
The main process rejected unknown/mismatched response identities and replayed/reordered child events.
The Harness child received only LANG, LC_ALL, NODE_ENV, and an E2E-only delay variable; ambient HOME, PATH, token, key, and secret variables did not cross the boundary.
Killing the utility process during a running step caused supervised restart and durable recovery to the creator approval gate.
Renderer reload, full application quit/relaunch, and packaged-ASAR launch retained or recovered the canonical run through narrow preload methods without exposing the MessagePort or database path.

limitations: The idea transform is deterministic structure, not research, evidence acquisition, model generation, or proof that the idea is commercially strong.

Only one zero-cost local loop and one interrupted-running-step recovery path are proven; the general graph compiler, external job reconciliation, budgets, cancellation, compensation, scheduling, and transition chaos matrix are not.
Node still labels node:sqlite experimental/release-candidate; it is isolated behind EventStore and must remain replaceable without changing canonical event/recovery semantics.
No signed, hardened, notarized, stapled, or Gatekeeper-accepted release artifact exists.
